title: Trade, an Increasingly Important Pillar in Dhampur’s Economy
description: In the ancient times, trade was the primary economic activity which led to the establishment of urban settlements, where agricultural surplus was exchanged for manufactured or imported goods like iron, stone, wood, agricultural implements, textiles, spices, etc. Over time these small market-towns also became industrial centers where artisans and crafts persons produced a range of […] The post Trade, an Increasingly Important Pillar in Dhampur’s Economy appeared first on CPR.
